seoTitle: Insert Markdown From a URL in Confluence (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to insert Markdown from a URL in Confluence in 2026, pulling raw .md files from GitHub or GitLab and configuring auto-refresh settings.
intro: Manually copy-pasting readme files from a code repository breaks formatting and quickly goes stale. This guide shows how to fetch and render live Markdown content from an external URL directly inside a Confluence page in 2026.

steps.heading: Open the markdown macro
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/markdown` to deploy the markdown macro container.

steps.heading: Find your raw file URL
steps.body: Locate and copy the secure "Raw" URL path from a public or private repository like GitHub or GitLab.

steps.heading: Connect the source
steps.body: Paste the URL into the macro's source configuration input to establish the fetch.

steps.heading: Configure the viewer
steps.body: Set a custom height, refresh interval, and code syntax highlighting for the rendered block.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review the rendered layout and click Publish to make your syncing content live.

faq.question: Can I pull a readme file directly from GitHub or GitLab?
faq.answer: Yes, copying the repository's Raw file URL and pasting it into the macro's source field renders the markdown content directly.

faq.question: Will the embedded content update if the source file changes?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure automated refresh intervals so the macro re-fetches and re-renders the source periodically.

faq.question: Does the macro support code syntax highlighting?
faq.answer: Yes, the viewer options let you enable code syntax highlighting for embedded code blocks.

faq.question: Should I test the link before publishing to the whole team?
faq.answer: Yes, validate the URL in a private draft preview before publishing to ensure the document syncs cleanly.
